name: Awoke Vintage
vibe: Curated 60s–90s denim, jackets, accessories. Every piece feels chosen by someone who actually loves vintage fashion. Clean, boutique feel.
bestDay: Any evening — open daily 10am–9pm. Easy post-dinner wander on any NYC night

name: Raggedy Threads
vibe: Specialises in vintage tees, 40s–60s denim, workwear, military, knitwear. Rustic ranch, Soviet working-class chic, mid-century rust belt. Mostly menswear. Closest to your actual taste.
bestDay: Fri 19 (afternoon after Met/Studio Museum, or Sat 20 morning before Coney)

name: Friends NYC
vibe: Indie department store carrying vintage clothing + local designers, Lazy Oaf, HUF, Pendleton, and choice designer vintage. More lifestyle/gifts mixed in. Friendly, creative vibe.
bestDay: Any quiet Bushwick morning — literally on your doorstep. Sat 20 or Sun 21 pre-Mister Sunday

name: OPC (Other People's Clothes)
vibe: Bigger dig, no-filler policy — creative chaos with the right payoff if you search. No fast fashion accepted.
bestDay: Any afternoon. Thurs 18 arrival evening if you land early enough

name: Monk Vintage
vibe: 90s and Y2K focus, big rotating inventory, bold colourful pieces. More volume than curation but prices around $25.
bestDay: Sat 20 or Sat 27 before Kim Petras — both walking distance

name: Grand Street Local
vibe: Strong focus on vintage streetwear, especially tees and sneakers. Good for band tees and graphic prints.
bestDay: Fri 19 or Fri 26 — pairs with Raggedy Threads as a Williamsburg sweep

name: Procell
hours: Mon–Sat: 12:00 PM – 7:00 PM | Sun: 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM
vibe: Hand-picked only, no bulk buying — streetwear, sportswear, designer, graphic tees all coexisting. The NYC benchmark.
bestDay: Fri 26 en route to Drag March — opens noon

name: Desert Vintage
vibe: No rummaging — 90s Margiela, 80s Montana, 2000s Miyake, all pristine. More elevated.
